## ADMC Mapping and Inertial Correspondence

The positive oriented momentum introduced above preserves the complete inertial information carried by $M$ and signed $p_k$. The relation is linear and exactly invertible. Applied along three independent directions, it connects the M1 formulation directly to special-relativistic four-momentum.

### Opposite-orientation map

For any oriented unit direction $\hat{k}$,

$$
p_{\hat{k}}^\oplus=M+\tfrac12p_k.
$$

Reversing the orientation changes $p_k$ to $-p_k$ while leaving $M$ unchanged:

$$
p_{-\hat{k}}^\oplus=M-\tfrac12p_k.
$$

The two readings form the linear map

$$
\begin{pmatrix}
p_{\hat{k}}^\oplus\\
p_{-\hat{k}}^\oplus
\end{pmatrix}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
1 & \tfrac12\\
1 & -\tfrac12
\end{pmatrix}
\begin{pmatrix}
M\\
p_k
\end{pmatrix}.
$$

Its inverse is

$$
\begin{pmatrix}
M\\
p_k
\end{pmatrix}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\tfrac12 & \tfrac12\\
1 & -1
\end{pmatrix}
\begin{pmatrix}
p_{\hat{k}}^\oplus\\
p_{-\hat{k}}^\oplus
\end{pmatrix},
$$

or equivalently,

$$
M=\tfrac12\left(p_{\hat{k}}^\oplus+p_{-\hat{k}}^\oplus\right),
\qquad
p_k=p_{\hat{k}}^\oplus-p_{-\hat{k}}^\oplus.
$$

The average recovers the positive core momentum. The difference recovers the signed component. The opposite-orientation readings and the pair $(M,p_k)$ are therefore two exactly equivalent representations of the same directional kinematic content.

### Conservation consequences

For an isolated system, ADMC conserves

$$
\mathcal P_{\hat{k}}^\oplus
=
\sum_i p_{\hat{k}}^{\oplus,(i)}
$$

for every oriented unit direction $\hat{k}$. It therefore also conserves the reading for $-\hat{k}$. Applying the inverse map to the two system readings gives

$$
\sum_i M^{(i)}
=
\tfrac12\left(
\mathcal P_{\hat{k}}^\oplus
+
\mathcal P_{-\hat{k}}^\oplus
\right),
$$

and

$$
\sum_i p_k^{(i)}
=
\mathcal P_{\hat{k}}^\oplus
-
\mathcal P_{-\hat{k}}^\oplus.
$$

Thus ADMC implies conservation of total $M$ and total signed momentum along $k$. Applied along three independent spatial directions, it recovers conservation of total three-momentum together with total $M$. Conversely, conservation of total $M$ and the signed components preserves the positive oriented readings. At the inertial level, the two conservation formulations are equivalent.

### Four-component form

Choose a Cartesian basis with oriented unit directions $\hat{x}$, $\hat{y}$, and $\hat{z}$. The M1 quantities can be assembled into the four-component package

$$
P_\oplus
=
\begin{pmatrix}
M\\
p_{\hat{x}}^\oplus\\
p_{\hat{y}}^\oplus\\
p_{\hat{z}}^\oplus
\end{pmatrix}.
$$

The corresponding signed package is

$$
P_M
=
\begin{pmatrix}
M\\
p_x\\
p_y\\
p_z
\end{pmatrix}.
$$

They are related by the invertible transformation

$$
P_\oplus=TP_M,
\qquad
T=
\begin{pmatrix}
1 & 0 & 0 & 0\\
1 & \tfrac12 & 0 & 0\\
1 & 0 & \tfrac12 & 0\\
1 & 0 & 0 & \tfrac12
\end{pmatrix},
$$

with

$$
P_M=T^{-1}P_\oplus,
\qquad
T^{-1}=
\begin{pmatrix}
1 & 0 & 0 & 0\\
-2 & 2 & 0 & 0\\
-2 & 0 & 2 & 0\\
-2 & 0 & 0 & 2
\end{pmatrix}.
$$

In component form,

$$
p_x=2\left(p_{\hat{x}}^\oplus-M\right),
\qquad
p_y=2\left(p_{\hat{y}}^\oplus-M\right),
\qquad
p_z=2\left(p_{\hat{z}}^\oplus-M\right).
$$

The four entries of $P_\oplus$ are a coordinate package for one momentum configuration, not four contributions to a single directional ADMC sum. Exact invertibility means that $P_\oplus$ and $P_M$ contain the same four degrees of inertial kinematic information.

### Correspondence with special relativity

Standard relativistic energy is introduced through

$$
E=cM.
$$

The signed package then becomes

$$
P_M
=
\begin{pmatrix}
E/c\\
p_x\\
p_y\\
p_z
\end{pmatrix}
=P^\mu,
$$

which is the standard special-relativistic four-momentum in a Cartesian inertial frame. The full correspondence is therefore

$$
P_\oplus=TP^\mu,
\qquad
P^\mu=T^{-1}P_\oplus.
$$

The M1 directional-positive package and special-relativistic four-momentum are related by an exact change of representation. The transformation does not discard a sign or introduce an additional degree of freedom; it reorganizes the same inertial momentum content.

The invariant relation agrees as well. M1's inertial composition rule gives

$$
M^2=p_f^2+p^2,
$$

and $p_f=m_0c$. With metric signature $(+,-,-,-)$,

$$
P^\mu P_\mu
=M^2-p^2
=p_f^2
=m_0^2c^2.
$$

Equivalently,

$$
E^2=m_0^2c^4+p^2c^2,
$$

the familiar special-relativistic energy-momentum relation.

The correspondence is exact at the inertial level. Opposite positive-oriented readings recover $M$ and each signed component $p_k$; three independent components assemble into four-momentum; and the M1 composition rule reproduces the special-relativistic invariant. M1 differs in its foundational ordering: it begins with positive momentum contributions in oriented directions and reaches the familiar four-component formulation through the invertible map.
